title: Antisemitic Threats and the Complex Journey of IVF
content:
Navigating the complex journey of infertility often brings unexpected emotional challenges from well-meaning friends and acquaintances. People frequently ask couples how many rounds of IVF they have endured, as though there were some hierarchy of effort or a scoreboard of grief. Furthermore, the timing and setting of these deeply personal questions can often be wildly inappropriate. In one instance, an acquaintance casually offered condolences about a miscarriage while standing in line at a buffet. The individual blithely added that they were sure a successful pregnancy would happen eventually.

While such sentiments are usually well-intentioned, being caught completely off-guard in a public setting is less than ideal. Grieving parents are forced to manage their profound loss while simultaneously navigating the awkward social dynamics of unsolicited advice. At a time when they desperately need exactly the right words and genuine empathy, they instead receive careless platitudes. These interactions highlight the widespread societal discomfort and ignorance surrounding the painful reality of infertility. Ultimately, it underscores the need for greater sensitivity and boundaries when discussing such intimate struggles.
